perturbation.original_text: Network Affiliate shall use commercially reasonable efforts to provide NCM at least six (6) months advance written notice (or such lesser time for notice as may be practicable based upon the date of execution of the agreement for such disposition and the disposition date) of the sale or other disposition of a Theatre, the loss of any Theatre lease, or its desire to permanently discontinue delivery of the Service to a Theatre (collectively, a "Disposition"). Subject to the provisions of Section 2.3 with respect to transfer of title to NCM Equipment, at least thirty (30) days prior to any Disposition, NCM shall be permitted to enter the affected Theatre(s) and remove any NCM Property. Except in connection with a Disposition, Network Affiliate shall not be permitted to permanently discontinue Service to any Theatre without the prior written consent of NCM, which consent will not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed. The costs of removal of NCM Equipment from any Theatre to which Service has been permanently discontinued shall be borne by Network Affiliate.
perturbation.changed_text: Network Affiliate shall use commercially reasonable efforts to provide NCM at least six (6) months advance written notice (or such lesser time for notice as may be practicable based upon the date of execution of the agreement for such disposition and the disposition date) of the sale or other disposition of a Theatre, the loss of any Theatre lease, or its desire to permanently discontinue delivery of the Service to a Theatre (collectively, a "Disposition"). Subject to the provisions of Section 2.3 with respect to transfer of title to NCM Equipment, at least fifteen (15) days prior to any Disposition, NCM shall be permitted to enter the affected Theatre(s) and remove any NCM Property. Except in connection with a Disposition, Network Affiliate shall be permitted to permanently discontinue Service to any Theatre without the prior written consent of NCM, which consent will not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed. The costs of removal of NCM Equipment from any Theatre to which Service has been permanently discontinued shall be borne by NCM.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the notification period for NCM to enter and remove property upon a Disposition. Initially, NCM had thirty (30) days prior to any Disposition. Now the altered version is fifteen (15) days. Additionally, the costs of removal initially borne by Network Affiliate, are now borne by NCM, making it unclear which party is responsible for these costs, this is creating an ambiguity. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty, as it's unclear what is the valid number of days, 30 or 15. Therefore impacting Network Affiliate responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Network Affiliate shall use commercially reasonable efforts to provide NCM at least six (6) months advance written notice (or such lesser time for notice as may be practicable based upon the date of execution of the agreement for such disposition and the disposition date) of the sale or other disposition of a Theatre, the loss of any Theatre lease, or its desire to permanently discontinue delivery of the Service to a Theatre (collectively, a "Disposition"). Subject to the provisions of Section 2.3 with respect to transfer of title to NCM Equipment, at least fifteen (15) days prior to any Disposition, NCM shall be permitted to enter the affected Theatre(s) and remove any NCM Property. Except in connection with a Disposition, Network Affiliate shall be permitted to permanently discontinue Service to any Theatre without the prior written consent of NCM, which consent will not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed. The costs of removal of NCM Equipment from any Theatre to which Service has been permanently discontinued shall be borne by NCM.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because the original text initially gave NCM thirty (30) days prior to any Disposition to enter and remove property, whereas the changed text reduces this to fifteen (15) days. Additionally, the original text stated that the costs of removal would be borne by Network Affiliate, which now in the changed text is borne by NCM. Since the changed text and the contradicted text are the same text, then there is an in-text contradiction according to the prompt. This difference in the notification period and which party bears the costs creates a direct conflict within the contract and is therefore a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For each twelve-month period following the Effective Date during the Term, and as long as Network Affiliate's attendance base in the Theatres for the twelve (12) month period is equal to or greater than 400,000 patrons (the "Base Amount"), the amount paid by NCM pursuant to Section 7.1(b) shall be not less than $ .17 per Theatre patron during such period with such amount increasing by 5% on each anniversary of the Effective Date (the "Minimum Fee"). The Minimum Fee shall be prorated to account for (i) any periods during which Network Affiliate's annual attendance base in the Theatres is lower than the Base Amount, and (ii) reductions in revenue associated with Network Affiliate's rejection of content as permitted under Section 3.4. Any payments made in order to satisfy the "Minimum Fee" which can be characterized as an advance of amounts due from advertising clients which is "earned but not yet paid" shall be deducted from the following year's payments when such amounts have in fact been collected.
perturbation.changed_text: For each twelve-month period following the Effective Date during the Term, and as long as Network Affiliate's attendance base in the Theatres for the twelve (12) month period is equal to or greater than 500,000 patrons (the "Base Amount"), the amount paid by NCM pursuant to Section 7.1(b) shall be not less than $ .20 per Theatre patron during such period with such amount increasing by 2% on each anniversary of the Effective Date (the "Minimum Fee"). The Minimum Fee will not be prorated under any circumstances. Any payments made in order to satisfy the "Minimum Fee" which can be characterized as an advance of amounts due from advertising clients which is "earned but not yet paid" shall not be deducted from the following year's payments.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the conditions for the Minimum Fee. Initially, the attendance base was 400,000, now it is 500,000. The amount paid by NCM was $0.17, now it is $0.20, and the amount increased by 5%, now is 2% on each anniversary, and the minimum fee will not be prorated, however initialy the minimum fee shall be prorated to account for (i) any periods during which Network Affiliate's annual attendance base in the Theatres is lower than the Base Amount, and (ii) reductions in revenue associated with Network Affiliate's rejection of content as permitted under Section 3.4. Finally payments will not be deducted from the following year's payments, however initialy Any payments made in order to satisfy the "Minimum Fee" which can be characterized as an advance of amounts due from advertising clients which is "earned but not yet paid" shall be deducted from the following year's payments when such amounts have in fact been collected. The contradiction will cause uncertainly on how the the calculation of revenue distribution works. And whether the payments are prorated or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For each twelve-month period following the Effective Date during the Term, and as long as Network Affiliate's attendance base in the Theatres for the twelve (12) month period is equal to or greater than 500,000 patrons (the "Base Amount"), the amount paid by NCM pursuant to Section 7.1(b) shall be not less than $ .20 per Theatre patron during such period with such amount increasing by 2% on each anniversary of the Effective Date (the "Minimum Fee"). The Minimum Fee will not be prorated under any circumstances. Any payments made in order to satisfy the "Minimum Fee" which can be characterized as an advance of amounts due from advertising clients which is "earned but not yet paid" shall not be deducted from the following year's payments.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text because it modifies several key terms, including the attendance base required for the minimum fee, the amount paid per patron, the rate of increase, and whether the minimum fee will be prorated. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text, but the original text, outlined in the prompt, had different numbers and proration conditions. These alterations create a strong inconsistency in calculating revenue distribution and whether payments are prorated, resulting in in-text contradiction.
